fixes_applied.original_issue: From<io::Error> discarded ErrorKind â€” all io::Errors became the opaque AvaError::IoError(String) variant, losing machine-readable kind information and preventing retryability detection for NotFound/PermissionDenied/TimedOut errors
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Replaced single-arm impl with a match on err.kind(): NotFoundâ†’AvaError::NotFound, PermissionDeniedâ†’AvaError::PermissionDenied, TimedOut|WouldBlockâ†’AvaError::TimeoutError (retryable), all other kindsâ†’AvaError::IoError (legacy fallback). Added doc-comment table in the impl. Also added deprecation doc comments on all 10 legacy string-payload variants (ToolError, IoError, PlatformError, ConfigError, NotFound, PermissionDenied, SerializationError, ValidationError, DatabaseError, TimeoutError) with migration guidance pointing to the structured variants. Updated test_error_conversions to assert the new variant mappings and updated it to also verify TimedOutâ†’TimeoutError is_retryable().

fixes_applied.original_issue: ProjectState::save() returned Result<(), String> â€” a bespoke error type that callers had to treat as an opaque string, not composable with the rest of the codebase's AvaError infrastructure
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Changed return type from std::result::Result<(), String> to ava_types::Result<()>. Replaced .map_err(|e| e.to_string()) chains with direct AvaError::from conversions (create_dir_all and fs::write now propagate through From<io::Error> which preserves ErrorKind) and an explicit AvaError::SerializationError for the serde_json call. Both existing call sites (ava-tui agent.rs and ava-agent stack/mod.rs) use let _ = state.save(...) so no call-site changes were needed.

fixes_applied.original_issue: run_validation_tool() returned Result<ToolResult, String> via .map_err(|e| e.to_string()), erasing the structured AvaError type. format_validation_result() accepted the same String-error Result, discarding error category and retryability.
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Changed run_validation_tool() return type from Result<ToolResult, String> to ava_types::Result<ToolResult> and removed the .map_err(|e| e.to_string()) call â€” the underlying tools.execute() already returns ava_types::Result so the error propagates naturally. Updated format_validation_result() signature to accept ava_types::Result<ToolResult>; the Err arm uses error.to_string() only for the human-readable unavailability message displayed in the post-edit validation section.

fixes_applied.original_issue: Two LazyLock<Regex> statics (RE_DIFF_STAT, RE_ISSUE) used .unwrap() â€” invalid regex patterns would panic at runtime on first use rather than failing fast during cargo test
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Added #[test] fn regexes_compile() in the existing tests module that forces initialisation of both RE_DIFF_STAT and RE_ISSUE via .as_str(). The LazyLock closures call Regex::new(...).unwrap(), so any invalid pattern causes the test to panic with a clear failure message during cargo test rather than silently deferring to first runtime use.

fixes_applied.original_issue: read_tool_errors_on_missing_file test used .contains("not found") (lowercase) which broke when From<io::Error> started mapping NotFound errors to AvaError::NotFound whose Display is 'Not found: ...' (capital N)
fixes_applied.fix_applied: Updated assertion to .to_ascii_lowercase().contains("not found") for case-insensitive matching, with an improved failure message showing the actual error string. This is a test maintenance fix required by the From<io::Error> improvement above.

summary: Four Round-2 error-handling focus items addressed with zero regressions. (1) From<io::Error> now preserves ErrorKind: NotFoundâ†’AvaError::NotFound, PermissionDeniedâ†’AvaError::PermissionDenied, TimedOut/WouldBlockâ†’AvaError::TimeoutError (retryable), everything elseâ†’IoError legacy fallback. All 10 legacy string variants received deprecation doc comments with migration guidance. (2) ProjectState::save() changed from Result<(), String> to ava_types::Result<()> â€” AvaError::from conversions now propagate structured errors from create_dir_all/fs::write. (3) run_post_edit_validation/run_validation_tool chain now uses ava_types::Result<ToolResult> throughout â€” map_err(|e| e.to_string()) eliminated. (4) regexes_compile() test added to ava-praxis::review catches LazyLock<Regex>.unwrap() panics at cargo-test time. One downstream test (read_tool_errors_on_missing_file) updated to case-insensitive matching to accommodate the From<io::Error> improvement. All suites green.
